title: Swinney would 'consider' banning US military from Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: First Minister John Swinney has said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft from using Prestwick Airport if it was confirmed…

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Swinney would 'consider' banning US military from Prestwick Airport John Swinney would 'consider' banning US military aircraft from Prestwick Airport (Image: Archive)

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald. First Minister John Swinney has said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft from using Prestwick Airport if it was confirmed they were involved in strikes in the Middle East.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney would 'consider' banning US military aircraft from Prestwick Airport
